_description: Verified language paper exam structures per board. NEVER estimate — all data from actual exam papers or official specifications.

_question_types.multiple_choice: Student selects correct answers from a list (e.g., AQA P2 Q1: choose 4 true from 8)
_question_types.retrieval: Short factual answer, 1-2 marks, single sentence
_question_types.analysis: 10-15 marks, multi-paragraph analytical response with evidence
_question_types.evaluation: 15-20 marks, extended evaluative response requiring personal judgement
_question_types.extended_writing: 20-40 marks, full essay structure (intro + body + conclusion)
_question_types.comparison: Cross-text comparison requiring references to both sources
_question_types.choice: Student chooses one task from multiple options

questions.description: List 4 things from lines X-Y
How does the writer use language to...
How has the writer structured the text to...
To what extent do you agree...
Descriptive or narrative writing (choice of 2 tasks)

grade_boundaries._note: Reference year fixed at June 2023 per product decision. Values pending Neil's fact-check against official AQA boundary document.

questions.description: Choose 4 true statements from 8 about Source A
Write a summary of differences/similarities using both sources
How does the writer use language to...
Compare how the writers convey different perspectives on...
Transactional writing (article, letter, speech, etc.)

language_c1.source: EDUQAS C700U10 specification + past papers (verified by Neil 2026-04-02)

questions.description: List five things you learn about X in these lines
What impressions does the writer create of X? Refer to language.
In what ways does X change? How does the writer show these changes?
What are the narrator's thoughts and feelings? How does the writer show them?
To what extent do you agree with this statement?
Creative prose writing — choice of 4 titles/prompts. Aim for 450-600 words.

language_c2.source: EDUQAS C700U20 past paper (verified by Neil 2026-04-02)

questions.description: Single fact retrieval from Source 1
Single fact retrieval from Source 1
Single fact retrieval from Source 1
How does the writer try to show X? Comment on language, tone, structure.
Single fact retrieval from Source 2
Single fact retrieval from Source 2
Single fact retrieval from Source 2
To what extent do you agree with this statement about Source 2?
Using both texts, explain briefly X (comparison retrieval)
Compare the impressions the writers create of X and Y
Transactional writing task 1 (article, guide, etc.). Aim for 300-400 words.
Transactional writing task 2 (letter, speech, etc.). Aim for 300-400 words.
Identify a word which shows X
Give two ways the writer shows X
How does the writer use language and structure to show X?
Evaluate how successfully the writer creates X. Support with detailed reference.
Choice of 2 imaginative writing tasks (narrative or descriptive)
Identify two activities / facts from Text 1
Give two tasks / details from an extract of Text 1
Analyse how the writer uses language and structure to interest and engage the reader
Identify one feeling / fact from Text 2
Identify one reason / fact from Text 2
Evaluate how successfully the writer demonstrates X
What similarities do the two people share?
Compare how the writers present ideas and perspectives
Choice of 2 transactional writing tasks (article, letter, guide, speech)

language_c1.source: OCR J351/01 specification + June 2024 past papers

sections.label: Section A: Reading Information and Ideas

questions.description: Retrieval from Text 1 — phrases and reasons
Cross-text comparison — what are the similarities?
How does the writer use language and structure to...
Evaluative cross-text comparison — how far do you agree?

sections.label: Section B: Writing for Audience, Impact and Purpose

questions.description: Choice of 2 transactional writing tasks (speech, letter, article, etc.)

language_c2.source: OCR J351/02 specification + June 2024 past papers

sections.label: Section A: Reading Meaning and Effects

questions.description: Retrieval from Text 1 — words/phrases and explanation
How does the writer use language and structure to show X? (Text 1)
Explore how the writer uses language and structure to... (Text 2)
Evaluative cross-text comparison — how far do you agree?

sections.label: Section B: Writing Imaginatively and Creatively

questions.description: Choice of 2 imaginative/creative writing tasks (story, descriptive, personal)

language_u1.source: CCEA GEN11 specification + Summer 2025 past papers
language_u1.note: CCEA uses Tasks not Questions. Writing FIRST, then Reading. 150-mark scale.

questions.description: Persuasive speech writing on a given topic

sections.label: Section B: Reading
sections.note: Reading texts printed INLINE in question paper (not separate insert)

questions.description: How has the writer presented X to engage readers? (non-fiction)
Structured comprehension — reasons + supporting evidence
How has language been used to develop X? (media text)
Presentational features — identify and explain intended effects

language_u4.source: CCEA GEN41 specification + Summer 2025 past papers
language_u4.note: Writing FIRST, then Reading. 150-mark scale. Choice of personal or creative writing.

questions.description: Choice: (a) personal writing OR (b) creative writing (image-prompted)
Compare and contrast how writers create X in two literary extracts
How has the writer gained and held the reader's interest? (non-fiction)
How has the writer presented their views on X? (same article continued)

language_p1.source: Edexcel IGCSE 4EA1/01 Spec A + June 2024 past papers

questions.description: Select two words or phrases that describe X
In your own words, describe what happens
Explain the writer's thoughts and feelings
How does the writer use language and structure to interest and engage?
Compare how the writers present their ideas and perspectives
Choice of 2 transactional writing tasks (review, article, speech, letter). Edexcel IGCSE 4EA1 uses AO4 (content/style) + AO5 (technical accuracy).

language_p2.source: Edexcel IGCSE 4EA1/02 Spec A + June 2024 past papers

questions.description: How does the writer present X? (single anthology poem, printed in full)
Choice of 3 imaginative writing tasks (personal, title-based, image-prompted). Edexcel IGCSE 4EA1 uses AO4 (content/style) + AO5 (technical accuracy).

language_p1.source: CAIE 0500/11 First Language English + June 2024 past papers
language_p1.note: 3 texts on separate Insert. Answer ALL questions. No dictionaries.

questions.description: Comprehension on Texts A+B (retrieval, own words, reasons) + summary (15 marks, 120 words max)
Language analysis on Text C — word identification, own words, feelings, extended analysis (200-300 words)
Role-play directed writing based on Text C (report, letter, article, etc., 250-350 words)

language_p2.source: CAIE 0500/21 First Language English + June 2024 past papers

questions.description: Directed writing article based on two texts (250-350 words)
Choice of 4: 2 descriptive + 2 narrative compositions (350-450 words)
